question: How many accessions were recorded in the ledger?
reference_answer: Eight accessions: FB-2026-001 through FB-2026-008

question: Which accession was deaccessioned at intake?
reference_answer: FB-2026-007 (Carex vulpinoidea, fox sedge) — condition D at intake, deaccessioned immediately as non-viable

question: Who collected FB-2026-003?
reference_answer: Dr. Rina Matsuda

question: What vault is FB-2026-004 stored in?
reference_answer: Vault 2 (wildflowers and forbs)

question: What is the corrected collection site for FB-2026-001?
reference_answer: Section 12 (corrected from initially recorded Section 11, per Dr. Matsuda's field notes)

question: What is the corrected seed count for FB-2026-005?
reference_answer: 6,800 (corrected from initially recorded 6,500, per Okoye's collection sheet)

question: What was the germination rate for FB-2026-003?
reference_answer: 38% — below the 40% threshold

question: What condition was FB-2026-003 reclassified to after viability testing?
reference_answer: Condition D — the 38% germination rate is below the 40% threshold for mandatory reclassification

question: Which accessions have not yet been viability tested?
reference_answer: FB-2026-005 (big bluestem, due May 11) and FB-2026-008 (American basswood, due June 6)

question: When is the viability test deadline for FB-2026-005?
reference_answer: May 11, 2026 — 90 days from the February 10 accession date

question: Why was the Transfer T-003 request denied?
reference_answer: Lot FB-2026-004 has only 2,100 seeds and is rated condition C. Bank policy requires maintaining at least 1,500 seeds for condition C lots. Transferring 1,000 would leave 1,100, below the threshold.

question: How many seeds from FB-2026-002 remain in storage after Transfer T-001?
reference_answer: Approximately 8,050 (8,500 original minus 450 transferred)

question: Who authorized Transfer T-001?
reference_answer: Bank Director Helena Voss

question: Which vault would a cryogenic seed lot be assigned to?
reference_answer: Vault 4 — cryogenic storage is assigned to Vault 4 regardless of species type

question: Why was FB-2026-006 split to Vault 4?
reference_answer: Conservation measure — the species (Baptisia australis, blue false indigo) is regionally declining and the curator wanted a backup in case of a Vault 2 refrigeration event. The germination rate was good (68%).

question: Is the Vault 4 split a viability concern?
reference_answer: No — the curator explicitly states the split is a conservation measure, not a viability concern

question: What germination rate threshold triggers reclassification to condition D?
reference_answer: Below 40% germination rate

question: Which accessions are currently in Vault 2?
reference_answer: FB-2026-002 (Rudbeckia hirta, ~8,050 remaining), FB-2026-004 (Asclepias tuberosa, 2,100), and FB-2026-006 (Baptisia australis, ~1,250 remaining after cryo split)

question: What is the current status of FB-2026-003?
reference_answer: Failed viability testing (38% germination, reclassified to D), scheduled for deaccession at the next monthly review. Not yet formally deaccessioned as of April 30.

question: Is there any evidence that FB-2026-005 is non-viable?
reference_answer: No — the viability test has not been performed yet. The lot is rated A at initial condition. No evidence of non-viability exists.

question: What is the minimum seed retention threshold for condition C lots?
reference_answer: 1,500 seeds

question: Which collector has the most accessions?
reference_answer: Dr. Rina Matsuda with three accessions (FB-2026-001, FB-2026-003, FB-2026-006). Marcus Okoye also has three (FB-2026-002, FB-2026-005, FB-2026-008). It is a tie.

question: What species is FB-2026-006?
reference_answer: Baptisia australis (blue false indigo)

question: Has the bur oak lot been deaccessioned yet?
reference_answer: Not yet — it is scheduled for deaccession at the next monthly review but has not been formally deaccessioned as of April 30

question: If FB-2026-005 fails viability testing with a 35% germination rate, what would happen?
reference_answer: It would be reclassified to condition D (below 40% threshold) and scheduled for deaccession
